insights.title: Discover wetlands
insights.description: Dive into different topics that define these unique ecosystems and uncover their significant impact on our environment.

hero.title: The dynamic ecosystems where water meets land.
hero.description: Ranging from mangroves and peatlands to rivers, lakes, floodplains, and coasts. Though they cover only about 6% of Earth's surface, but their impact is immense. Wetlands are indispensable for climate stability, biodiversity, and human livelihoods.

climate-resilience.title: Wetlands capacity to stabilise local climates and hydrological systems make them indispensable allies in the fight against climate change.
climate-resilience.description: Wetlands are efficient natural carbon sinks, for example peatlands cover only 3% of the global land area, but store twice as much carbon than all the world’s forests combined. Coastal wetlands such as mangroves also trap significant amounts of blue carbon and buffer coastlines from storm surges and sea-level rise.

biodiversity-strongholds.title: Nearly 40% of all species rely on wetlands at some point in their life cycle
biodiversity-strongholds.description:
Wetlands rank among the most biologically diverse ecosystems on Earth. They provide vital habitat for an immense variety of species, birds, amphibians, fish, invertebrates, and plants.

Freshwater wetlands, in particular, are home to about half of all fish species, despite covering less than 1% of the earth’s surface.

stats.1:

(since 1970 we loss 81%)<sup>2</sup>

engines-for-economic-development.title: Wetlands deliver ecosystem services worth up to $39 trillion annually.
engines-for-economic-development.description:
Wetlands hold immense economic value, yet they are often overlooked. Over a billion people rely on them for farming, fishing, and ecotourism.

Healthy wetlands reduce poverty, enhance food security, and boost economies. Investing in them is crucial for both the environment and the economy.

stats.1:
of global GDP is estimated to come from wetland
ecosystem services<sup>1</sup>

wetlands-under-threat.title: Since 1970 we’ve lost 35% of wetlands—3× faster than forests<sup>1</sup>.
wetlands-under-threat.description:
Despite their vital roles, wetlands are disappearing at an alarming rate.

Peatlands are being drained and burned, mangroves are being cleared for aquaculture and tourism, and river floodplains are being cut off by dams and levees. As wetlands vanish, we lose not only biodiversity and ecosystem services, but also the safety nets that protect the most vulnerable people from environmental end economic shocks.
wetlands-under-threat.sup: 1. Ramsar Global Wetland Outlook, 2018 | 2. Global Wetlands Outlook, 2025

landscapes.title: A lifeline in the Sahel
landscapes.description:
The Sahel region of Africa underscores the importance of wetlands. In this dry area, seasonal wetlands and floodplains provide fertile land for farming, pastures for livestock, and fish for communities.

They also support cultural traditions and social ties.

As the Sahel faces climate change and conflict, protecting wetlands is crucial for regional stability.

footer.description: The Gap Map platform was created to support policy makers, communities and investors to act on this urgency. The platform serves as a one stop shop to provide these users with the information they need to make decisions on where to focus their actions. This is done by integrating the latest and most innovative remote sensing work within the Sahel and combining this with easy to digest insights and stories on the different vital roles wetlands play and where they are located.

header.title: The Importance of Wetlands
header.description: Wetlands are among the most productive and threatened ecosystems on Earth, yet decisions about their future are often constrained by incomplete data and an information mismatch.

the-gap-map.title: The Gap Map
the-gap-map.description:
The Gap Map bridges diverse data sources and tells compelling stories to highlight the value of wetlands, making it easier to target investments,guide policy advocacy, and build stronger collaborations across sectors and regions.

Built on open-access principles, it brings scattered data together into one easy-to-use platform, showing where wetlands are, why they are important, and where conservation and restoration efforts can have the greatest impact.

explore.title:
Explore,
discover, act
explore.description:
Users can explore the current state of knowledge, discover where gaps exist, and connect to iconic wetland landscapes.

Continuously updated with input from global partners, the platform reflects the latest science and practice, helping guide decisions for a sustainable future.
